commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=458367ee93a8a42c66d15375aca6504816435c09)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds opt-in AI session title generation, persists `autoTitle`, projects `derivedTitle` into session-list/UI paths, and widens runtime model-auth lookup to accept session profile parameters.

PR surface: Source +353, Tests +223. Total +576 across 24 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. for the linked feature request itself, but the PR defect is source-reproducible: a single 8192-byte read can skip a long first JSONL record and keep counted user turns below the configured threshold.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow code/test repair can fix the transcript scanner; maintainers still own the config, privacy, and auth-profile decision after that mechanical blocker is resolved.

target.title: fix(sessions): auto-generate session titles via AI summarization
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Session titles were added as default-on background summarization, but review found upgrade/privacy risk, missing auth-profile propagation, and generated titles overriding existing subject labels.
- Solution: Make AI session titles explicit opt-in, carry the resolved session auth profile into title generation, and keep existing manual/subject naming precedence ahead of generated titles.
- What changed: Updated the session title generator, conversation label auth lookup, runtime model-auth wrapper, session title precedence, config help text, and focused regression coverage.
- What did NOT change: No dependency changes. Manual session renaming still wins, existing subject labels still win, and title generation still uses the selected session model only when sessionTitle.enabled is true.

Fixes #77165

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** model-generated session titles must be opt-in, use the session-scoped auth profile for the background model request, persist autoTitle, and surface the generated title through the session-list/sidebar data path without hiding manual names or subject labels.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux x86_64, Node 24.16.0, local OpenClaw PR worktree for the submitted PR diff; the final rebase only changed commit SHA and the PR diff was verified unchanged before push. Gateway startup used `pnpm gateway:dev` through the dev-gateway script with DeepSeek configured. Title persistence/sidebar proof used the patched OpenClaw session-title generator, session store, runtime model-auth lookup, and `sessions.list` row builder against a local OpenAI-compatible endpoint.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** 
```bash
OPENCLAW_REPO=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-87643 \
  OPENCLAW_LOG=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-87643-evidence/dev-gateway-current-head.log \
  OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN=proof-token-87643 \
  OPENCLAW_MODEL=openai/deepseek-chat \
  bash /media/vdc/code/ai/Hermes/scripts/dev-gateway-start.sh

TASK_WORKTREE=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-87643 \
  env -C /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-87643 \
  PATH="/home/0668001030/.config/nvm/versions/node/v24.16.0/bin:$PATH" \
  node --import tsx /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-87643-evidence/session-title-runtime-proof.mts

env -C /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-87643 \
  PATH="/home/0668001030/.config/nvm/versions/node/v24.16.0/bin:$PATH" \
  NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 \
  pnpm exec vitest run \
    src/auto-reply/reply/conversation-label-generator.test.ts \
    src/auto-reply/reply/session-title-generator.test.ts \
    src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts \
    src/infra/bonjour-discovery.test.ts \
    src/plugins/runtime/runtime-model-auth.runtime.test.ts \
    ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts

env -C /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-87643 PATH="/home/0668001030/.config/nvm/versions/node/v24.16.0/bin:$PATH" pnpm check:test-types
env -C /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-87643 PATH="/home/0668001030/.config/nvm/versions/node/v24.16.0/bin:$PATH" pnpm lint --threads=8
```
- **Evidence after fix:** 
```text
2026-05-29T12:37:25.224+08:00 [gateway] agent model: openai/deepseek-chat (thinking=medium, fast=off)
2026-05-29T12:37:25.227+08:00 [gateway] http server listening (9 plugins: acpx, browser, canvas, device-pair, file-transfer, memory-core, openai, phone-control, talk-voice; 6.3s)
2026-05-29T12:37:25.234+08:00 [gateway] ready
OpenClaw session title runtime proof passed
local OpenAI-compatible endpoint requests: 1
auth profile used for title request: openai:default (user locked)
autoTitle persisted: Invoice Export Duplicate Tax Rows
sessions.list sidebar derivedTitle: Invoice Export Duplicate Tax Rows
subject precedence row: Existing Subject Label
manual displayName precedence row: Manual Sidebar Name
Test Files  8 passed (8)
Tests  357 passed (357)
$ pnpm tsgo:test
$ pnpm tsgo:core:test && pnpm tsgo:extensions:test
$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo
$ node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --threads=8
[oxlint:core] starting
[oxlint:core] still running after 30s
[oxlint:core] still running after 60s
[oxlint:core] finished
[oxlint:extensions] starting
[oxlint:extensions] still running after 30s
[oxlint:extensions] still running after 60s
[oxlint:extensions] finished
[oxlint:scripts] starting
[oxlint:scripts] finished
```
- **Observed result after fix:** On current HEAD, `sessionTitle.enabled` remains opt-in. With `sessionTitle.enabled: true`, the title generator used the session model and user-locked auth profile, persisted `autoTitle`, and `sessions.list` exposed the generated `derivedTitle` for sidebar display. Existing `subject` and manual `displayName` still take precedence over generated titles. The PR-focused tests, `pnpm check:test-types`, and `pnpm lint --threads=8` passed after the rebase/conflict fix.
- **What was not tested:** No live browser screenshot was captured; the sidebar behavior was verified through the same `sessions.list` row data consumed by Control UI. The title proof used a local OpenAI-compatible endpoint for deterministic model output; gateway startup was verified separately with the DeepSeek model configuration.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: Unit and local runtime proof
- Target test file: src/auto-reply/reply/session-title-generator.test.ts; src/auto-reply/reply/conversation-label-generator.test.ts; src/plugins/runtime/runtime-model-auth.runtime.test.ts; src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts
- Scenario locked in: sessionTitle is opt-in, title generation carries the resolved auth profile, runtime model auth receives profile parameters, autoTitle persists into the session store, and subject/displayName remain stronger than autoTitle.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: These tests cover the exact review blockers at the narrow functions that schedule title generation, resolve auth, and compute sidebar title precedence.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: The first implementation treated missing `sessionTitle` config as enabled, forwarded provider/model without the auth profile used by the reply, and inserted `autoTitle` before `subject` in the title precedence chain.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Existing tests covered generated title display and model override routing, but not opt-in upgrade behavior, auth-profile propagation, or subject-over-autoTitle precedence.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
